Home / Hourly vs Fixed Rate Calculator

Hourly vs Fixed Rate Calculator

Calculate the hourly rate, monthly retainer, and project equivalents needed to reach your annual income goal.

How to Use This Tool

1
Enter your target annual income goal.
2
Set your realistic billable hours per week (most freelancers bill 20-30).
3
Enter how many weeks you plan to take off per year.
4
Review your required hourly, daily, weekly, and monthly retainer rates.
5
Check the assessment to see if your target rate is realistic for your market.
6
Scroll down to compare what different income goals require from your pricing.

Frequently Asked Questions

How many billable hours per week is realistic?

Most full-time freelancers bill 20-30 hours per week. The remaining time goes to admin, marketing, proposals, learning, and breaks. If you are billing 40 hours every week, you are likely burning out. Plan for 25 billable hours as a sustainable target.

Should I charge hourly or fixed-price?

Fixed-price is generally better for experienced freelancers. Clients prefer predictable costs, and you earn more when you work efficiently. Use hourly for unclear scopes or ongoing retainer work. Start with hourly, then transition to fixed as you learn to estimate accurately.

What if my target hourly rate is too high?

You have three levers: increase billable hours, lower your income goal, or specialize in a higher-paying niche. Working with US/EU clients while living in a lower-cost region is a proven strategy to make a high effective rate achievable.

Should I charge different rates to different clients?

Yes. Early clients might get a lower rate for testimonials. Enterprise clients should pay premium rates. Long-term retainer clients can receive a 10-20% discount. The key is to never disclose your rate variance — each client gets a custom quote based on project scope.

How do I factor in taxes and business expenses?

Multiply your desired take-home income by 1.3-1.5 to cover self-employment taxes (15.3% in the US), health insurance, software, hardware, and professional development. If you want $100K take-home, target $130K-$150K in gross revenue.

Results

Copy All
Example

💼 Pricing Model Calculator

🎯 Your Income Target • Annual Income Goal: $100,000 • Billable Hours/Week: 30 hrs • Weeks Off Per Year: 4 wks • Working Weeks: 48 wks • Annual Billable Hours: 1,440 hrs 💵 Required Pricing • Required Hourly Rate: $69.44/hr • Daily Rate (8 hrs): $556/day • Weekly Rate: $2,083/wk • Monthly Retainer: $8,333/mo 📈 Ambitious but realistic. $69.44/hr requires solid expertise and client trust. Specialize deeply to justify this rate.
